outcome: variant A: dropped cached intermediate, used new_var = arg0 first. Build mismatch: 49 vs 49 length, 3 structural + 9 rename diffs. GCC scheduler emits move at insn idx 2 (right after stack save), target needs it at idx 12 in the lw load-delay slot.
variant D: late declaration new_var = arg0 placed AFTER v0_tmp lw. GCC eliminated the move entirely as dead — mine became 48 insns vs target 49.

tools/dc.sh permute func_800483DC --max-time 300 --max-flat 60. 16-thread run, 1463 iterations, plateaued at score 30 with reason=flat. Permuter mutation operators (decl reorder, intermediate vars, expression restructure) cannot shift GCC allocator off the s2/s3/s4 cycle.
changed signature to s32 *base, added explicit s32 *list = base save var. Same 12 diffs (3 structural + 9 rename). GCC allocator still picks s2 for list, not s4 like target.
added volatile to v0_tmp and the table pointer cast. WORSE: 52 vs 49 (3 extra insns), 7 structural + 8 rename diffs. volatile adds memory roundtrip per access.

borrowed sibling func_800481E8 INLINE_MOVE_ALIASING shape (asm-pin cached=arg0 upfront, plain C new_var=cached). WORSE: 25 diffs total (12 structural + 13 rename) vs variant A baseline of 12. The opaque move forces an extra reg materialization that cascades.

added __asm__("" ::: "memory") between lw_v0_tmp and new_var assignment. Same 11 diffs as variant D (48 vs 49, move eliminated). The empty barrier does NOT preserve the dead move; GCC still dead-elims it.
added cached intermediate variable but no pin/asm. Same 11 diffs as variant D (48 vs 49, move eliminated). The cached aliasing doesn't help — GCC sees through it to arg0.

permuter --max-time 600 --max-flat-seconds 180 single-seed run. 3687 attempts in 266s, terminated reason=flat at score 30. Same plateau as short permuter_seed run with default flags. 3x more iterations did not reduce score, confirming permuter mutation space exhausted.
